summary: claw_messenger postinstall silently global-installs unpinned third-party packages and registers a root/admin-persistent system service
details: On npm install, dist/postinstall.js performs two unrelated, unpinned global package installations and escalates to root/admin privileges to register a persistent boot-time service. (1) A detached `npm install -g opencode-ai` is run against `registry.npmmirror.com` with no version pin and no user prompt; opencode-ai is an unrelated third-party AI coding agent published by a different maintainer, is not mentioned in README, and its mutable @latest content will execute under the user's account whenever opencode is invoked. (2) dist/github-downloader.js runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest`, then dist/service-installer.js uses `pkexec`/`sudo` on Linux, `osascript... with administrator privileges` on macOS, and PowerShell `Start-Process -Verb RunAs` on Windows to register `claw-subagent-service --install` as a systemd/launchd/Windows boot-persistent service. Because both fetched packages are pinned to mutable `@latest`, whoever controls those package names can ship arbitrary code that subsequently executes on every installer's machine — and on Linux, runs as root via the registered service. The harm fires automatically from npm's postinstall lifecycle without user opt-in. A separate disclosed flow (auto-register.js posting MAC address + hostname to https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register on plugin load) is documented in README and is a transparency concern but not the basis for this verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
